title: Th'iruckai

The '''Th'iruckai''' were a primitive, [[Reptile/Legends|reptilian]], [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] from the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Th'irus]]. They were a warlike species who had a vast knowledge of poisons. Their society was organized into tribes led by [[King/Legends|kings]] called [[Teruk]]s. When explorers from other species reached Th'irus, the Th'iruckai tribes fought [[Blood/Legends|bloody]] battles over the advanced technology brought to their world. A group of offworld [[Smuggler/Legends|smugglers]] eventually learned that the Th'iruckai fought because of an ancient misunderstanding and began mediating a ceasefire between the clans.

==Biology and appearance==
The Th'iruckai were an [[Sentience/Legends|intelligent]] [[Species/Legends|species]] native to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Th'irus]]. They were short, [[Reptile/Legends|reptilian]] bipeds.<ref name="SWGS" />

==Society and culture==
The Th'irukai were organized into tribes, with a [[Teruk]], or [[King/Legends|king]], as the leader. The species was extremely warlike, developing [[Sword/Legends|swords]], [[Mace (weapon)/Legends|maces]], and [[Bow/Legends|bows]] with their [[Bronze/Legends|bronze]]-age technologies. Consequently, they were keen for advanced military technology available from offworld sources once [[Smuggler/Legends|smugglers]] arrived on the planet. The Th'irukai also waged war with poisons and neurotoxins, and, consequently, had advanced medical techniques to counter those threats. The Th'irukai domesticated [[bathalz']], creatures that closely resembled [[Bantha/Legends|banthas]].<ref name="SWGS" />

==History==
The Th'iruckai evolved on Th'irus, a [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="SWGS" /> located in [[Wild Space/Legends|Wild Space]].<ref name="TEAOC">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}} — Based on corresponding data for [[Th'irus system/Legends|Th'irus system]]</ref> It was a hot, [[Desert/Legends|desert]] world, with virtually no surface [[Water/Legends|water]]. They banded together into tribal nations, and after a misunderstanding between several Teruks, a thousand-year war broke out between the various polities. That state of affairs continued until the planet was discovered by the wider [[The galaxy/Legends|galaxy]]. A group of [[Smuggler/Legends|smugglers]] then traveled to the world, bringing with them advanced weaponry. Many of the tribes were keen to obtain these armaments so as to secure a significant advantage over their rivals. Consequently, the smugglers were contacted by a number of tribes, immediately causing a battle to break out.<ref name="SWGS" />

{{RPGseed}}
The battle underway, [[Oolan Krim]], the Teruk of the [[Gallzar clan]], arrived with a significant force and beat back his rivals. He then invited the smugglers back to his village for rest, recuperation, and trade talks. Meanwhile, Krim had his warriors drag the smugglers' [[Starship/Legends|starship]] into the desert to use as a bargaining chip in case they did not wish to trade with him.<ref name="SWGS" />

[[Hurn Jak]], the leader of a competing clan, also tried to exert pressure on the smugglers to trade the weapons to him. Having infiltrated the Gallzar clan with some of his loyal agents, Jak had the smugglers inflicted with [[Yurfloo/Legends|yurfloo]], a slow-acting poison. As Jak was the only one with the antidote, the smugglers would be forced to deal with him to save their lives. Faced with those difficulties, the smugglers negotiated an understanding with the competing Th'iruckai nations, uncovering the misunderstanding that caused the thousand-[[Standard year/Legends|year]] war in the first place.<ref name="SWGS" />
{{Endgame}}

==Behind the scenes==
The Th'iruckai were created by [[Bill Olmesdahl]] for the ''[[Star Wars Gamemaster Screen]]'', a [[West End Games]] product for the second edition of their [[roleplaying game]]. Published in [[1992]], the book contained a number of adventure hooks that were designed to be fleshed out by game masters. The Th'iruckai adventure was titled ''[[Hostile Natives Of Th'irus]]'' and was classed as a smuggler adventure. In addition to the main proposed storyline, presented above, Olmesdahl proposed an alternative version wherein the war between the clans was of more recent origin and was started by offworld [[Merchant/Legends|traders]] wanting to stimulate an arms trade.<ref name="SWGS" />
